text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这是美国之音慢速英语健康报道节目。

text: COPD, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease, blocks air flow through the lungs.
zh: COPD，即慢性阻塞性肺病，会阻塞肺部的气流。

text: It makes breathing difficult.
zh: 它会使呼吸变得困难。

text: The leading cause is cigarette smoking.
zh: 其主要病因是吸烟。

text: Experts at the National Institutes of Health in the United States say the damage to the lungs cannot be repaired and there is no cure.
zh: 美国国立卫生研究院的专家表示，肺部一旦受损便无法修复，也没有治愈方法。

text: Don DeMeo is an assistant professor at Harvard Medical School in Boston, Massachusetts.
zh: 唐·德梅奥是马萨诸塞州波士顿哈佛医学院的助理教授。

text: Dr. DeMeo says by 2020
zh: 德梅奥博士说，到2020年，

text: COPD will likely be the third leading cause of death across the world.
zh: COPD很可能成为全球第三大致死原因。

text: COPD is a new name for emphysema and chronic bronchitis.
zh: COPD是肺气肿和慢性支气管炎的新名称。

text: These are the two most common forms of the disease.
zh: 这是该疾病最常见的两种类型。

text: Many Many people with COPD have both of them.
zh: 许多COPD患者同时患有这两种病症。

text: And Dr. DeMeo says more women than men now die from the disease.
zh: 德梅奥博士还指出，如今死于该病的女性人数已超过男性。

text: She is the lead author of a study by a team from Harvard's Brigham and Women's Hospital and the University of Bergen in Norway.
zh: 她是哈佛大学布里格姆妇女医院与挪威卑尔根大学联合研究团队的这项研究的主要作者。

text: way.
zh: 途径。

text: The new study adds to findings that women may be more at risk than men for the damaging effects of smoking.
zh: 这项新研究进一步证实了此前的发现，即女性可能比男性更易受吸烟危害的影响。

text: The team examined results from a Norwegian study of 954 people with COPD.
zh: 该团队分析了一项挪威研究的结果，涉及954名COPD患者。

text: Inga Cecilia Sorheim co -authored the team's findings.
zh: 英加·塞西莉亚·索尔海姆是该团队研究成果的合著者之一。

text: Dr. Sorheim says they show that women suffered the same severity of COPD as men,
zh: 索尔海姆博士说，研究显示女性COPD的严重程度与男性相当，

text: but by comparison, the female smokers were younger and had smoked a lot less.
zh: 但相比之下，女性吸烟者更年轻，吸烟量也少得多。

text: The team also looked at two groups among the people in the study.
zh: 该团队还对研究对象中的两个群体进行了分析。

text: These were people under the age of 60 and those who had smoked for for less than 20 years.
zh: 分别是60岁以下人群，以及吸烟不足20年的人群。

text: In both cases, women had more severe COPD and a greater loss of lung function than men.
zh: 在这两种情况下，女性的COPD都比男性更严重，肺功能损失也更大。

text: Dr. DeMaio says some of the people in the study did not smoke much, but still developed severe lung disease.
zh: 德梅奥博士说，研究中有些人吸烟并不多，却仍然患上了严重的肺病。

text: She said, many people underestimate the health risks of their own cigarette smoking.
zh: 她说，很多人低估了自己吸烟对健康的危害。

text: They think that a few cigarettes every day are harmless.
zh: 他们以为每天抽几支烟没什么害处。

text: But clearly there is no such thing as a safe level of cigarette smoke exposure.
zh: 但事实很明显，吸烟暴露并不存在所谓的“安全水平”。

text: And our findings suggest that this is especially true for women.
zh: 我们的研究结果表明，这一点对女性尤其如此。

text: Dr. Sorgheim says there are several possible explanations why women may be more at risk from the effects of cigarette smoke than men.
zh: 索尔海姆博士说，关于为何女性可能比男性更易受香烟烟雾影响，有几种可能的解释。

text: Women have smaller airways, she says, so each cigarette may do more harm.
zh: 她说，女性的气道更细，因此每支香烟造成的伤害可能更大。

text: Also, there are differences between males and females in the way the body processes cigarette smoke and she says
zh: 此外，男性和女性在身体代谢香烟烟雾方式上也存在差异，她还表示，

text: genes and hormones could also play an important part and that's the VOA special English health report
zh: 基因和激素也可能在其中发挥重要作用，以上就是本期美国之音慢速英语健康报道节目的内容。
